reference: I was wondering if you could start by telling me what brought you into the emergency Department. So I came to the emergency Department because I've been having pain in my chest. OK, where is the pain located? I would say it's like the whole pretty much the left side of my chest. OK, and when did this pain start? So I think it started just around 2 hours ago. I actually was riding my bicycle and just fell off on, kind of just slipped and I think I fell on that side like when I the impact was right on my chest and since then it's been really painful. OK I am how would you describe the pain, sometimes people will use words like burning dull, achy. I think it's more like someone is just stabbing me and then whole left side area. OK, and um. Anything that you've been doing that's that's made the pain worse? If I. Think I have to take really really slow and shallow breaths. If I try to take a deep breath it just really hurts like taking those small, smaller breaths helps. OK, and do you feel the pain anywhere else, or is it just on the left side of your chest? No, I would say just on the left side. OK, and has the pain been getting worse, staying the same or getting better? I'd say over the last two hours it's been getting worse. OK, and do you have any other associated symptoms? Uh, like I think I I'm having. Also maybe it's just because I'm trying to breathe slowly, but I feel like I can't catch my breath. OK, um has anything like this ever happened to you before? No, no. I mean, I've never had any pain in my chest before. OK are you, did you have a loss of consciousness when you fell off your bike? No, I got right back up. I just just had the pain. OK, so any injuries to the head? No. OK. That's fine. I was wearing a helmet. OK, excellent and any headache right now? No. OK, how about any fever chills? No, I feel I'm fine. Any nausea or vomiting? Nope. Do you feel as though your heart's racing? I would say like in the last hour a little bit more so than usual. OK. Any skin changes or or rashes you've noticed? Besides, just I got some scrapes here and there on my hands, but nothing else. OK, and do you have a cough? Nope. OK, and any recent infections? No, I've been pretty pretty healthy recently. No, no cough or no infections. OK, and uh, any dizziness? Um no. Alright, and I know you mentioned the few scratches on your hands, but any other injuries? Oh no, I I think I got a pretty lucky. Oh, OK. And any alcohol or drug intake prior to falling off your bike? Oh no, no, I was completely sober. No no drugs or alcohol. OK, and any weakness or numbness or tingling that you noticed? Um no nothing like that. Alright, and, when was the last time you had anything to eat or drink? I just had some cereal and, um, just some chocolate milk around breakfast time but nothing else. OK, and how many hours ago was that? I would say around 3 hours ago. OK. Um, and have you been diagnosed with any medical conditions in the past? Uh, not other than just a hay fever, but nothing nothing else. I have like a lot of allergies. Any allergies to medications? Oh, just sometimes when I take like a nasal spray I think I might have an allergy to the steroids that they give me and sometimes also some of the antihistamines I think I get some allergies from them. OK. Uh, and any hospitalizations? No, never. Any surgeries? Nope. OK. Anybody in the family who have any illnesses? Just my father. He has type 2 diabetes but nothing else. OK, and how do you support yourself currently financially? Oh so I work just as like a mechanic just in the city and currently I just have myself to support. I live alone in an apartment, so I'm doing fairly well. OK, and do you smoke? I smoke around like I'd say 2 and a half to 3 packs per day. I know it's. Yeah, I have tried quitting before, but I would say I've been smoking since I was 18. OK, and do you smoke marijuana? No, no, no marijuana, no other drugs. OK, and do you drink alcohol? Yeah, it's just like sometimes on the weekend. I'll just have a beer or two while watching the game, but not nothing regular. OK, thank you.
